tasks.task: Optimize Prompt Generator System Instruction for Pixel Art
tasks.description: Update the GeminiAdapter system_instruction to constrain generated prompts for pixel-art-friendly compositions: centered character, simple or transparent background, clear outlines, front or 3/4 view.

steps.description: Read current system_instruction in src/modules/prompt_gen/gemini_adapter.py
Replace system_instruction with pixel-art-optimized version emphasizing centered subject, simple background, clean outlines, limited detail
Add pixel-art constraint guidance to the PromptOutput style_parameters schema documentation
Update unit tests to verify the new system instruction content

tasks.acceptance-criteria: system_instruction contains pixel-art-specific constraints: 'centered', 'simple background' or 'transparent background', 'clear outlines', 'front view' or '3/4 view'. Generated PromptOutput.positive_prompt includes pixel-art-friendly directives. All existing prompt_gen tests pass.

unit: system_instruction string contains 'centered' and 'simple background' or 'transparent background' keywords
system_instruction string contains 'pixel art' and 'clean outlines' keywords
_construct_request returns config with updated system_instruction (mocked client)
All existing prompt_gen unit tests still pass without modification

tasks.description: Create a new pre_processing module with background removal on high-res image, subject-aware cropping, and LANCZOS downscale to intermediate resolution (default 256px) before pixelization.

steps.description: Create src/modules/pre_processing/ directory with __init__.py, schemas.py, interface.py, exceptions.py
Define PreProcessingInput schema with fields: image_path, remove_background, intermediate_size (default 256), crop_mode
Define PreProcessingOutput schema with fields: image_path, original_size, intermediate_size
Implement PreProcessor class with process() method: load image, optionally remove background, crop to square, downscale to intermediate_size using LANCZOS, save and return
Implement subject-aware cropping: use alpha channel bounding box when available, fallback to center crop
Create unit tests under tests/modules/pre_processing/

tasks.acceptance-criteria: PreProcessor.process takes a high-res image path and returns a path to an intermediate-resolution square image. When remove_background=True, background is removed before cropping. Subject-aware crop uses alpha bounding box to center the subject. LANCZOS resampling produces smooth intermediate image. intermediate_size defaults to 256. All tests pass.

unit: PreProcessingInput accepts valid fields with defaults: intermediate_size=256, crop_mode='center', remove_background=True
PreProcessingInput rejects intermediate_size <= 0
process() with remove_background=True calls BackgroundRemover.remove (mocked) then crops and downscales
process() with remove_background=False skips background removal, performs crop and downscale only (mocked image)
Subject-aware crop extracts alpha bounding box and centers subject in square canvas (synthetic RGBA test image)
Subject-aware crop falls back to center crop when image has no alpha channel (synthetic RGB test image)
Downscale to intermediate_size uses LANCZOS resampling and outputs exact square dimensions (synthetic image)
process() saves output image to expected path and returns correct PreProcessingOutput fields

tasks.description: Tune the ComfyUI workflow template for low-resolution pixel art conversion: reduce denoise to 0.32, lower ControlNet strength to 0.65, and make the positive prompt dynamically injected with pixel-art prefix.

steps.description: Update workflow_api_template.json: set denoise to 0.32, ControlNet strength to 0.65
Replace hardcoded positive prompt text with a generic placeholder that will be overridden at runtime
Update negative prompt to include 'deformed, bad anatomy, extra limbs, mutation' for character preservation
Update ComfyUIAdapter._build_workflow to always inject the upstream prompt into the positive text node
Add pixel-art prefix to injected prompt: prepend 'pixel art, clean edges, limited palette, ' before the upstream prompt
Update existing pixelization unit tests for new parameter values

tasks.acceptance-criteria: workflow_api_template.json has denoise=0.32 and ControlNet strength=0.65. Positive prompt node text is overridden at runtime by prepending 'pixel art, clean edges, limited palette, ' to the upstream prompt. Negative prompt includes anatomy-preserving terms. All pixelization tests pass.

unit: _build_workflow sets denoise to 0.32 in KSampler node when no override provided (loaded template)
_build_workflow sets ControlNet strength to 0.65 in ControlNetApply node (loaded template)
_build_workflow injects 'pixel art, clean edges, limited palette, {prompt}' into positive CLIPTextEncode node when prompt is provided
_build_workflow preserves default positive text when prompt is None
Negative prompt node contains 'deformed' and 'bad anatomy' keywords (loaded template)
All existing pixelization unit tests pass without modification

tasks.task: Fix Post-Processing Pipeline Order: Downscale Before Quantize
tasks.description: Reverse the post-processing step order from quantize-then-downscale to downscale-then-quantize, ensuring color palette is applied at final target resolution with no sub-pixel color mixing.

steps.description: In PostProcessingPipeline.process(), move the downscale loop before color quantization
Apply color quantization individually to each downscaled image at target resolution
Remove background removal from PostProcessingPipeline since it is now handled in pre-processing stage
Keep remove_background field in schema for backward compatibility but log a deprecation warning when used
Update unit and integration tests to verify new order: downscale first, then quantize

tasks.acceptance-criteria: PostProcessingPipeline processes each target size by: (1) downscale to target_size, (2) quantize at target resolution. Color quantization happens on the small image, guaranteeing no intermediate colors from resampling. Background removal in PostProcessingPipeline logs deprecation warning. All pipeline tests pass with new order.

unit: process() calls downscaler.downscale BEFORE color_quantizer.quantize for each target size (mocked, verify call order)
process() calls quantize on each individual downscaled image, not on the original large image (mocked, verify arguments)
process() with remove_background=True logs deprecation warning and still calls BackgroundRemover (mocked)
process() with remove_background=False does not call BackgroundRemover (mocked)
Output images at each target size have exactly palette colors after quantization (synthetic test image with known palette)
integration: Full post-processing chain with real Downscaler and ColorQuantizer: 256px input downscaled to 32px then quantized to sweetie-16 palette has at most 16 unique RGB colors
Full post-processing chain: 256px input downscaled to 64px then quantized to gb palette has at most 4 unique RGB colors

tasks.description: Update the FullPipeline orchestrator to run pre-processing between IMAGE and PIXELIZATION stages, feeding the 256px image into ComfyUI instead of the original 1024px image.

steps.description: Add PreProcessor as a dependency to FullPipeline.__init__
Add intermediate_size field to FullPipelineConfig with default 256
Insert pre-processing call after IMAGE stage output: remove background, crop subject, downscale to intermediate_size
Pass pre-processed 256px image path to PIXELIZATION stage instead of original 1024px image
Disable remove_background in POST_PROCESSING stage since it is now handled in pre-processing
Update orchestrator unit tests to verify pre-processing is called between IMAGE and PIXELIZATION

tasks.acceptance-criteria: FullPipeline.run inserts pre-processing between IMAGE and PIXELIZATION. Pre-processing removes background, crops subject, and downscales to intermediate_size (default 256). PIXELIZATION receives 256px image. POST_PROCESSING receives ComfyUI output without re-running background removal. All orchestrator tests pass.

unit: run with start_stage=PROMPT calls pre_processor.process after image_generator and before pixelizer (all mocked, verify call order)
Pre-processor receives image_generator output path and remove_background=True from config (mocked)
Pixelizer receives pre_processor output path (256px), not image_generator output path (1024px) (mocked)
Post-processing is called with remove_background=False regardless of config.remove_background (mocked)
FullPipelineConfig accepts intermediate_size=256 as default
run with start_stage=PIXELIZATION skips pre-processing (mocked, verify pre_processor not called)
run with start_stage=POST_PROCESSING skips both pre-processing and pixelization (mocked)
integration: Orchestrator correctly constructs PreProcessingInput from real ImageGenOutput schema with remove_background=True
Orchestrator correctly passes real PreProcessingOutput.image_path to PixelizationInput

tasks.description: Run the optimized pipeline against real APIs and ComfyUI to validate improved pixel art quality at 32x32 and 64x64 target sizes.

steps.description: Run python main.py --prompt 'a pixel art anime girl with blonde hair full body standing pose' with valid API keys and running ComfyUI
Verify output/assets/ contains correctly sized assets and intermediate files show the new pipeline order
Compare old vs new results: check that character proportions are preserved and colors are strictly within palette
Run all pytest tests to confirm no regressions across the full test suite

tasks.acceptance-criteria: Full pipeline produces pixel art assets where: character is recognizable at 64x64. Colors are strictly within the chosen palette. Character proportions are preserved without deformation. All pytest tests pass.

e2e-manual: Run full pipeline with --prompt -> verify 32x32 and 64x64 PNG assets have recognizable character shape
Open output assets in image viewer -> verify all pixel colors belong to sweetie-16 palette
Compare intermediate images: pre-processed 256px image shows clean background removal and subject centering
Compare pixelized image: ComfyUI output at 256px preserves character proportions without deformation
Run pytest -> all unit and integration tests pass
